commit.message:
refactor: 重构 WAAPI 前后端分层与路由缓存策略

- 前端新增 features/waapi typed API 与通用 composables，移除页面重复逻辑
- 路由改为单一配置源，导航由路由驱动，按 meta.keepAlive 控制页面缓存
- Rust 将 lib.rs 拆分为 state/waapi_client/commands 模块并抽取公共调用逻辑
- 新增 parse_return_fields 与订阅重复开启相关单测
- 新增 pnpm check、.editorconfig，README 统一为 pnpm 工作流

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): promote UDP slave simulator to lib

Summary:
- move the simulated slave runtime from test support into
  lib/ethercat/simulator* as a supported simulator surface
- keep the deep integration suite on the real UDP transport and update
  tests/docs to the new EtherCAT.Simulator* modules
- add the simulator generalization plan and refresh the simulator
  README/spec notes for the new public layout

Rationale:
- the simulator is now intended for tooling such as kino_ethercat, so
  it needs a real library boundary instead of test-only support code
- compiling it from lib keeps the API available to consumers while the
  deep tests still exercise the same implementation
- the README and active plan now make the current coverage and missing
  features explicit before analog, servo, and DC profiles are added

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add 'git branch-dates'

commit.message:
feat(test): add simulator fault injection coverage

Summary:
- add simulator faults for dropped responses, wrong WKC, named slave
  disconnects, SAFEOP retreat, AL error latching, and mailbox aborts
- extend deep integration coverage for recovery, shared-domain reconnect,
  mailbox aborts, and slave-local SAFEOP retreat handling
- document the new Milestone 4 runtime and fault-injection behavior in
  the support slave reference docs

Rationale:
- deepen the UDP simulator so it can reproduce real recovery scenarios
  that were previously only observable on hardware
- keep the support slave aligned with the SOES-derived spec notes while
  making the integration test path a stronger regression oracle

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

commit.message:
feat(test): extend UDP slave simulator coverage

Summary:
- add richer support fixtures for coupler and LAN9252-style I/O rings
- add mailbox-capable simulator behavior with expedited CoE upload and
  download handling in PREOP
- extend deep integration coverage to heterogeneous rings and public
  SDO roundtrips over the real UDP transport
- update the simulator README and derived SOES notes to match the
  implemented Milestone 2 and Milestone 3 scope

Rationale:
- the simulator should cover more than the minimal one-byte digital I/O
  happy path before mailbox work begins
- deep tests need a protocol-faithful mailbox path so PREOP diagnostics
  and configuration can be exercised without hardware
- keeping the support docs aligned with the derived slave spec makes the
  simulator easier to evolve without drifting from its test-only role

Validation:
- mix format test/support/slave/mailbox.ex test/support/slave/device.ex
  test/support/simulator.ex test/ethercat/support_slave_device_test.exs
  test/ethercat/deep_integration_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

commit.message:
feat(test): add UDP slave simulator for deep tests

Summary:
- add compiled test support for simulated EtherCAT slaves and a
  multi-slave simulator behind a real UDP endpoint
- add deep integration tests that boot the normal master over the
  UDP transport and exchange cyclic I/O against one and two
  simulated slaves
- tighten UDP startup validation, ignore the vendored SOES tree,
  and document the simulator design against derived slave spec notes

Rationale:
- deep integration coverage needs a protocol-faithful peer that can
  exercise normal master startup and cyclic I/O without hardware
- loopback UDP keeps the test path end-to-end through the real bus
  and transport layers without requiring root privileges
- stricter AL transition handling and explicit slave spec notes make
  the simulator a stronger regression oracle instead of a happy-path
  echo fixture

Validation:
- mix format test/ethercat/deep_integration_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/deep_integration_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(cron): avoid false CUR resets and improve DB pipeline instrumentation

- refine CUR repeat detection to compare persisted rows
- harden cloudflare inserted detection path
- add DB/cron regression tests and timing logs
- bump app version and update changelog/worklog

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- 移除 .gitignore 中对 .codex-review 的忽略规则

原因:

- 为 automation/source/profile/flow 模型落地清理旧的 WORKFLOW.md 口径

验证:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: 支払い作成フォームで任意項目の送信に対応

カテゴリとメモを必須入力から外し、
日付・金額・カテゴリ・メモの順で入力できるように整理した。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix optional config metadata types

Align @optique/config's public types with runtime behavior.
bindConfig() key callbacks now receive metadata as
TConfigMeta | undefined, and ConfigLoadResult.meta uses the same
type for loaders that omit metadata.

Add regression coverage for both cases and update the config
integration docs to show safe narrowing when metadata is required.
Document the change in CHANGES.md.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/155

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): harden timeout contracts and dev release docs

Summary:
- bound plain Master API calls with a local call timeout and return
  {:error, :timeout} instead of exiting callers
- update public specs/docs for master-facing calls and subscribe/3 to
  reflect timeout behavior accurately
- clarify the 0.3.0-dev development line in README and CHANGELOG while
  keeping the latest Hex install snippet at 0.2.0
- add a regression test for EtherCAT.state/0 timing out cleanly when the
  master is suspended

Rationale:
- the await APIs already translated local call timeouts, but the rest of
  the public Master surface still used an unbounded call and could hang
  or exit callers under a wedged local master
- the public docs and types should match real timeout behavior so users
  and Dialyzer see the same contract
- the repo should clearly distinguish unreleased 0.3.0-dev work on main
  from the latest published Hex release

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/master/api.ex lib/ethercat.ex README.md CHANGELOG.md test/ethercat_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix nested list continuation indent

Use a shared helper for list-item continuation indentation so
paragraph wrapping and block serialization agree inside definition
list details. This fixes a false positive where nested ordered list
continuation lines lost one leading space on reformat, causing
`--check` and `--diff` to report already formatted files as dirty.

Add a minimal regression test for the nested ordered-list case and
record the fix in CHANGES.md.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: stabilize HUD layout and add gameplay screenshots

Major Features:
- Gameplay HUD foundation
  - Standardize the in-play HUD around fixed slots and transparent gutter-based placement
  - Add gameplay screenshot capture via \ with default F12 binding
  - Apply shared UI theme tokens across HUD and core menu/result screens

Minor Features:
- HUD readability
  - Move combo feedback to the bottom-center gutter
  - Rework debug overlay into a compact two-line bottom-right display
  - Make power-up timers grow downward from just below the top wall

Code Improvements:
- HUD/runtime safety
  - Add lifecycle-safe visual helper initialization for spawned balls to avoid multiball crash during early speed updates
  - Tighten HUD helper layout behavior for debug and timed power-up indicators

Documentation:
- UI overhaul tracking
  - Update .agent architecture, tech stack, and Tasks/Backlog/ui-overhaul.md to reflect completed HUD/theme groundwork and remaining overhaul work

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(release): 发布0.32.0并支持任务图片挂载

- 按 .slfk/docs/PLAN.md 完成图片附件能力基座，支持 URL/本地路径/base64 抽取、workspace 安全写入和失败分类回执
- 打通"任务增加图片"命令与 add_task_images tool，接入路由白名单与意图词典并同步 README
- 补齐命令解析、抽取注入、handler/E2E/失败路径回归测试，确保部分成功与并发写入场景稳定
- 更新 CHANGELOG.md 并将插件版本提升到 0.32.0（metadata.yaml/main.py）

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Propagate runParser callback exceptions

Remove the try/catch-based callback arity detection in runParser's
help, version, completion, and error paths. Callbacks now always
receive the numeric exit code, and real exceptions propagate instead
of being swallowed by a zero-argument fallback.

Update the facade regression tests to assert exception propagation
and record the behavior change in CHANGES.md.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/153

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(mermaid): align charts with current runtime

Summary:
- update public Master lifecycle and startup Mermaid charts to match
  the current state machine and recovery behavior
- expand the DC lock-state chart to include the real disabled and
  inactive states
- mark the old lifecycle alignment review diagrams as historical and
  add Mermaid rendering support to generated HTML docs

Rationale:
- several charts still implied outdated transitions like direct bus-down
  returns to :idle, which no longer match the runtime
- generated HexDocs should render the diagrams instead of showing raw
  Mermaid fences

Validation:
- mix format mix.exs lib/ethercat.ex lib/ethercat/master.md
- mix format lib/ethercat/dc.md
- mix format docs/design-docs/lifecycle-spec-alignment-review.md
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(bus): gate domain recovery on carrier state

Summary:
- add carrier-aware bus runtime info and expose link monitor mode in
  diagnostics
- stop domains immediately on confirmed :down and block stopped-domain
  restarts while carrier is absent
- replace the bus recovery test seam with a dedicated non-async test
  that registers a fake EtherCAT.Bus

Rationale:
- the previous recovery gating used the bus scheduler state, which let
  stopped domains churn restart->timeout->stop while the cable was
  physically unplugged
- recovery should follow actual carrier availability, and the runtime
  model should stay honest instead of pretending the bus server is
  configurable

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs
- real hardware unplug/replug on enp0s31f6

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(ui): build shared design system foundation

Summary:
- add a pnpm workspace and scaffold the tree-shakeable `@packtravel/ui`
  package with build, typecheck, and test scripts
- define PackTravel brand tokens and export a reusable Tailwind preset
  for downstream apps
- implement typed React primitives for forms, layout, navigation,
  tour cards, ratings, dialogs, and loading states with Vietnamese
  copy coverage
- update repo docs and agent validation guidance for the new frontend
  package workflow

Rationale:
- the ticket starts from a repo that only contained Symphony workflow
  scaffolding, so the shared UI package needed its own workspace and
  package infrastructure
- exporting tokens, preset, and components from one package creates a
  single design-system foundation that future apps can consume
- validation coverage with real Vietnamese strings gives direct
  evidence for the acceptance criteria around typography and rendering

Tests:
- pnpm validate

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: dependabot[bot] <49699333+dependabot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(main): 完成入口模块拆分并修复事件标准化回退

- 按 .slfk/docs/PLAN.md 拆分 main.py 入口职责，新增 handlers 与 services 运行时装配模块\n- 事件监听改为官方装饰器直连，统一 status 指令判定与 NormalizedEvent 字段回退逻辑\n- 新增 requirements.txt 并补充装饰器契约、运行时装配与事件标准化去重回归测试\n- 更新 CHANGELOG 并将 metadata.yaml 与插件注册版本升级到 v0.2.1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(cron,db): reduce DB round trips and add session cache refresh

- add in-memory session cache with DB refresh on session-related scan failures
- merge c2c upsert + inserted detection with RETURNING fallback
- write snapshot via INSERT..SELECT from product
- keep detail_blob write-only in async flush path
- bump versions to 0.9.5.5 and update changelog/tests

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): tighten dependency footprint wording

Summary:
- update the README and top-level EtherCAT moduledoc wording about
  runtime dependencies
- keep the user-facing message focused on the small runtime footprint
  without mentioning prior internal implementation choices

Rationale:
- end users care that the library stays lightweight, not which
  specific dependency was removed
- this keeps the docs accurate and cleaner to read

Validation:
- mix format README.md lib/ethercat.ex
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(bus): remove vintage_net dependency

Summary:
- remove the vintage_net dependency and its passive config
- add internal bus interface and link-monitor helpers based on
  sysfs and netlink, with polling fallback
- update raw socket transport, redundant link handling, examples,
  and tests to use the new bus-owned monitoring path

Rationale:
- keep link detection inside the bus subsystem instead of leaking a
  network-management dependency through transport and link code
- preserve link change notifications while keeping the library
  dependency-free at runtime

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs
- mix run examples/diag.exs --interface enp0s31f6
- mix run examples/probe.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --frames 2 --timeout_ms 200

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(runtime): clarify state-machine boundaries

Summary:
- update the maintainer and public docs to describe Master, Slave,
  Domain, and DC as state-machine modules with helper namespaces
- fix stale navigation and architecture references, including the
  ESC SII path and source-adjacent briefing entry points
- tighten the Domain and DC diagrams so they no longer imply extra
  gen_statem states that only exist as runtime data

Rationale:
- the recent refactors made the runtime shells much easier to audit
  against the EtherCAT model, but the docs still used vague "shell"
  wording and left several boundaries implicit
- clearer state-machine terminology makes the design easier to review
  without relying on project-specific jargon

Validation:
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): add codex tui notification config

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(master): move slave recovery mechanics out of shell

Summary:
- move reconnect authorization and PREOP-to-OP retry mechanics from
  EtherCAT.Master into EtherCAT.Master.Recovery
- drop direct Slave.API usage from the master state-machine shell
- keep master state clauses focused on event routing and transitions

Rationale:
- the shell should mention slave/domain/dc events as state-machine
  concepts, but it should not perform low-level subsystem operations
- moving the mechanics into Recovery makes the master easier to audit
  against the protocol lifecycle and removes duplicated reconnect logic

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/master.ex lib/ethercat/master/recovery.ex
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: batch DB writes, async blob pipeline, and traceable scan logs

- add per-scan trace id across scan and blob logs\n- decouple data phase from blob writes in cron flow\n- enforce blob concurrency limit/alert (10/8)\n- batch c2c/product/snapshot writes and single-request blob update\n- bump frontend version to 0.9.5.4 and update docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(examples): refresh maintained hardware scripts

Summary:
- update scan and SDO debug examples to use the current
  EtherCAT.Slave.ESC namespace
- fix loopback cleanup so it stops the linked bus cleanly instead of
  killing the caller process
- keep loopback focused on the EL2809 path by holding the RTD bridge in
  PREOP during activation

Rationale:
- the maintained examples drifted after the slave ESC refactor and the
  hardware pass exposed the stale module paths immediately
- loopback is meant to inspect EL2809 SII and LRW-facing state, so it
  should not fail just because the unrelated EL3202 cannot reach OP

Validation:
- mix format examples/scan.exs examples/sdo_debug.exs examples/loopback.exs
- mix test
- mix run examples/scan.exs --interface enp0s31f6
- mix run examples/sdo_debug.exs --interface enp0s31f6
- mix run examples/loopback.exs --interface enp0s31f6

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(release): 发布0.31.0并统一运行时端口与可观测性

- webui_runtime 默认端口统一为 API 18999 / WebUI 19000，并同步生产与本地开发脚本、Vite 代理和文档。\n- 修复 webui_runtime 启用配置兼容性，支持 enable/enabled 别名与 enable/disable 文本布尔值。\n- 增强运行时可观测性：回灌 API/WebUI 子进程 stdout/stderr，并补充启动失败错误日志。\n- 新增 runtime 分层回归与端口一致性契约测试，覆盖配置边界、生命周期分支和日志任务行为。

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add Symphony travel starter repo

Summary:
- add a standalone Symphony starter repo with WORKFLOW.md, AGENTS.md,
  a PR template, and a run helper
- copy the core commit, push, pull, land, and linear skills into the
  repo so unattended workflows have the expected local tooling
- make the starter portable by resolving Symphony via SYMPHONY_HOME
  instead of assuming it lives inside the parent checkout

Rationale:
- this creates a clean base repository that can be pushed and used as
  the starting point for a Travel project running the Symphony workflow
- keeping the workflow contract and skills inside the repo matches the
  Elixir README setup model and reduces manual bootstrap work

Tests:
- bash -n bin/run-symphony
- python3 -m py_compile .codex/skills/land/land_watch.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(mvp): 完成聊天工具平衡插件主链路落地

- 按 .slfk/docs/PLAN.md 落地阶段1~3核心能力与模块化目录结构\n- 实现图片OCR缓存、工具意图路由、主题归档、短期记忆与上下文构建\n- 接入总结触发与执行、LivingMemory v2同步及失败重试闭环\n- 增补主链路与降级路径测试，并更新 README/CHANGELOG/metadata 版本到 v0.2.0

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(command): 修复 /rss get 回退格式与分段输出

- 移除 /rss get 响应中的内部 mode 字段，仅返回可读正文
- smart provider 不可用时改为 smart 风格兜底排版（内容标题 + 链接列表 + 时间 + 摘要）
- 分段逻辑改为无损拆分，避免超长文本被首段截断后丢失后续内容
- 更新 README、契约测试与回归测试，并升级版本到 v0.7.4

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(command): 拆分 /rss get 长消息并避免触发转发

- get_output 渲染支持返回多段文本并按段输出
- /rss get 模式化输出在发送前按 600 字分段，避免超长单条回复触发转发链路
- 增加 smart->full 回退长文本拆段回归测试
- 升级版本到 v0.7.3 并更新 CHANGELOG

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Handle cancelled Inquirer prompts

Catch ExitPromptError from @inquirer/prompts and convert it
into a normal parse failure so ^C during an interactive prompt
does not escape as an unhandled rejection.

Add a regression test for prompt cancellation and document the
fix in CHANGES.md.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/151

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(push): 收敛转发兼容路径为直接发送

- 将 _send_forward_message 收敛为逐段文本直发并复用 send_text_message
- 将 _attempt_push_forward 发送模式日志统一为 direct_segments
- 更新 send_adapter 测试基线，移除 Node/Nodes 转发断言并覆盖直发分段场景
- 升级版本到 v0.7.2，并补充 CHANGELOG 记录

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Avoid withDefault help crashes

Catch function-based default failures during getDocFragments() so
help generation no longer throws. Skip evaluating doc-only defaults
when a custom help message is present, and add regression coverage for
both paths.

Update CHANGES.md to document the fix.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/150

commit.message:
fix(push): 改为直发文本并收敛 get 参数边界

- full/smart 发送前文本超过 600 字时按 ## 小节优先分组并截断，避免 forward 失败阻断推送。\n- full 推送统一走文本分段发送，保留分段延迟与可靠投递链路。\n- /rss get 默认 number 调整为 1，最大限制为 5，并更新参数错误提示。\n- 同步 README、CHANGELOG、metadata/main 版本到 v0.7.1，更新相关回归测试断言。

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix joined duplicate option detection

Align duplicate detection for joined option syntax with the existing
space-separated path so deferred and dependency-source states are
recognized consistently.

Add regression tests for repeated `--option=value` inputs with
`DerivedValueParser` and `DependencySource`, and document the fix in the
changelog.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/149

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(master): harden await and activation retry paths

Summary:
- reply blocked await_running callers when stopping during discovering
- keep activation_blocked distinct from recovering until activation
  failures actually clear
- handle reconnect authorization for activation-blocked slaves and
  expose timeout returns in Slave.API and Domain.API
- update specs and tests to match the low-level timeout behavior

Rationale:
- the new shell/API split left a few lifecycle edges inconsistent
- startup activation and runtime recovery should remain separate
  state-machine concerns
- low-level facades must not exit callers on gen_statem call timeouts,
  especially when Master uses them internally

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

commit.message:
refactor(runtime): separate master and slave shell APIs

Summary:
- move public master and slave call wrappers into dedicated API facade
  modules so the gen_statem files stay focused on lifecycle handling
- extract shared master active-state calls and PREOP slave
  configuration into helper modules and move slave init/polling setup
  into runtime helpers
- move master and slave lifecycle diagrams into source-adjacent markdown
  files and restore await_* timeout translation in Master.API with a
  regression test

Rationale:
- keeping public call plumbing out of master.ex and slave.ex makes the
  state machines easier to audit against the EtherCAT startup and ESM
  flows
- source-adjacent lifecycle docs preserve the diagrams while reducing
  noise in the state-machine modules themselves
- the timeout fix preserves the documented public contract for
  await_running/1 and await_operational/1 after the API extraction

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(mint-requests): hoist api helper mock for vitest

Summary:
- fix the new mint request api helper unit test to use vi.hoisted
  for the mocked api client
- validate the helper with a minimal temp vitest config backed by
  the backend vitest install while the root toolchain remains broken

Rationale:
- vitest hoists vi.mock factories, so the previous top-level const
  mock caused a real ReferenceError before the test body ran
- correcting the test keeps the frontend helper coverage executable
  even in the current partial root install state

Tests:
- node backend/node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config /tmp/fue11-root-helper-vitest.config.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(mint-requests): serialize mark-minted tx finalization

Summary:
- serialize mark-minted finalization by tx hash with a Postgres
  advisory lock inside the persistence transaction
- move duplicate tx-hash detection under that lock and keep the
  existing idempotent and mismatch responses for raced updates
- extend the route test to cover the transactional lock path

Rationale:
- review note parsing alone could reject duplicates only after the
  fact and left a race across concurrent requests using the same tx
- locking on the verified tx hash closes that replay window without
  requiring a blocked Prisma schema migration in this sandbox

Tests:
- ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/routes/mint-requests-mark-minted.test.ts tests/services/mintRequestVerification.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts
- npm run test (fails in sandbox: Supertest listen EPERM on 0.0.0.0; auth suite also requires prisma generate)
- npm run build (fails in sandbox/network: prisma generate needs schema engine from binaries.prisma.sh)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore: initialize repository

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(runtime): start 0.3.0-dev fault cleanup

Summary:
- classify PDO-participating slave_down faults as critical runtime
  faults while keeping non-critical slave faults local
- simplify Master and Domain fault handling around explicit
  classification and cycle-result categories
- add the active execution plan, update debt tracking, bump the
  project to 0.3.0-dev, and commit repo-managed git hooks

Rationale:
- the previous split between slave_faults and runtime_faults was
  directionally right but too coarse for PDO-participating slaves
- Domain should stay the authority for cyclic health while Master
  exposes a clearer operational versus recovering contract
- checking in .githooks makes the formatting and docs workflow part
  of the repo instead of hidden local setup

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(mint-requests): verify mark-minted transitions on-chain

Summary:
- require a verified tx hash before moving mint requests to minted
- verify chain, receipt, factory target, AssetCreated payload, and
  emitted asset bytecode against the approved request
- align the frontend mark-minted helper with the required tx hash and
  add focused route, service, and helper tests

Rationale:
- prevent backend approval state from drifting away from real on-chain
  mint execution
- keep duplicate and replay handling idempotent while rejecting
  unverifiable or mismatched transactions

Tests:
- cd backend && ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/routes/mint-requests-mark-minted.test.ts tests/services/mintRequestVerification.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts
- npm run typecheck
- npm run lint (fails: missing @eslint/js in incomplete root install)
- npm run test:unit (fails: node_modules/vitest/config missing)
- npm run build (fails: incomplete root type packages)
- cd backend && npm run test (fails: sandbox listen EPERM in existing Supertest suites)
- cd backend && npm run build (fails: prisma engine download blocked by restricted network)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(evals): Restore skill discovery and reaction replies (#81)

Restore the conversational eval paths that drifted after the recent
routing and credential changes.

The failing cases were a mix of real regressions and stale fixtures. The
eval harness was resolving extra skill directories from the repo root,
so the eval-only smoke skills no longer loaded. Monorepo runs also were
not discovering sibling workspace plugin packages, which meant the
GitHub and Sentry capability providers were missing even though those
plugin packages were present locally.

This updates the harness and local plugin discovery so the smoke flows
can load the expected skills and providers, aligns the affected eval
cases with the current user-visible contracts, updates `jr-rpc` guidance
to reflect `github.repo` fallback, and suppresses redundant thread text
when a reaction already acts as the acknowledgement.

I ran the targeted in-sandbox unit coverage and `skills:check` here. The
conversational evals still need an out-of-sandbox run because the repo
guidance says they are not reliable inside Codex.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

add external watermark publication and boundary-based Reclaim for Bedrock epoch aggregation

overload for externally-computed boundaries.

feat(workflow): add complexity scoring handoff gate

Summary:
- require a workpad-only complexity score after agent review and before
  Human Review
- add the scoring rubric, guardrails, and workpad template section to the
  crowd-snake workflow
- align README and AGENTS guidance with the new handoff order

Rationale:

  runtime behavior

Tests:

  during Postgres init)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(phase2): Behavioral ML Engine + WAF scoring server

Phase 2 Implementation:

INGRESS PROXY CHANGES
- telemetry_mirror.py: Add 6 session-level behavioral features
  (inter_request_interval_ms, uri_path_diversity, body_size_variance,
   http_method_diversity, ua_change_detected, param_count_variance)
  via rolling 60s per-IP session window
- ml_client.py: Add behavioral_score_request() — dual scoring
  (WAF + behavioral) with 50ms timeout and fail-open guarantee
- proxy_server.py: asyncio.gather() parallel WAF + behavioral scoring;
  deception_trigger overrides routing score to 100 (honeypot)
- config.py: Add BEHAVIORAL_SCORE_URL and BEHAVIORAL_TIMEOUT_MS env vars

ML SERVICE
- ml-service/app.py: FastAPI scoring server with POST /score,
  GET /health, GET /model/info
  - Loads isolation_forest.pkl + xgboost_classifier.pkl + feature_scaler.pkl
  - Score formula: (iso_norm * 0.6) + (xgb_attack_proba * 0.4) * 100
  - Always fail-open (HTTP 200 on any error)
- ml-service/training/train.py: Full training pipeline
  - Loaders: NSL-KDD + SQL dataset + XSS dataset + payload files
  - Vectorized pandas (no iterrows)
  - Numpy-based oversampling (sklearn 1.8 compatible, no imblearn)
  - Drops singleton classes before stratified split
- ml-service/models/model_metadata.json: Training metadata

CORE SERVICE
- core/behavioral_scorer.py: Isolation Forest pipeline
  (train/score/persist) with 15 behavioral FEATURE_COLS
  Intent levels: Benign / Exploratory / Malicious
- core/__init__.py: POST /api/behavioral/score endpoint (fail-open)
  and behavioral_scorer initialization
- core/drift_detector.py: Concept drift detection for model staleness
- core/train_behavioral.py: One-shot baseline training helper

Trained model metrics (97% accuracy):
  normal:  precision=0.98, recall=0.99
  sqli:    precision=0.83, recall=0.72
  xss:     precision=0.99, recall=0.98
  latency: <1ms per request

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(startup): use safer bus timeout floor

Summary:
- tune bus frame timeout with a 2ms host-jitter floor
- cap the automatic timeout at half of the smallest domain cycle
- cover the heuristic with focused startup tests

Rationale:
- avoid false-positive bus timeouts on non-RT Linux hosts running
  slower domain periods such as 10ms
- keep the timeout bounded so one late frame cannot consume most
  of a cycle budget

Validation:

- mix test

commit.message:
eventloops: Add poller-backed sleep APIs (#66)

* eventloops: Add poller-backed sleep APIs

Add a poller-managed sleep heap alongside fd deadlines so tasks can
block on timer wakeups without Base.Timer. Switch internal timeout and
sleep users to the new EventLoops helpers, keep the backend-only tests
on Base sleep/timedwait, and add direct coverage for the real poller
sleep path.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(eventloops): unify timer state

Replace the sleep-only state and heap entries with a unified timer
state that can support one-shot sleeps today and repeating timers
later. Keep the public sleep/timedwait behavior intact while using
, , , and  as the timer
lifecycle model, and guard timer arming against poller shutdown races.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(eventloops): unify time heap plumbing

Merge descriptor deadlines and timer wakeups onto a single `TimeEntry`
heap in `EventLoops` while keeping separate fire semantics for
`PollState` deadlines and `TimerState` wakeups. This removes the
duplicate heap, peek, drain, and wake-delay machinery and keeps the
internal deadline normalization helper and tests aligned with the
shared time-entry model.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(eventloops): distinguish ready and cancel wakes

Teach `PollWaiter` to carry a wake reason so I/O readiness can be
kept distinct from deadline and close wakeups. Use that in
`wait_read!` and `wait_write!` to retry internally on stale canceled
wakes while letting a latched readiness wake drive the next syscall,
which brings the behavior closer to Go's `pdReady` versus `pdNil`
semantics.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(internal-poll): stabilize stale wake retry test

Widen the initial deadline and observation window in the new stale
canceled wake test so it reliably exercises the retry path on
single-threaded CI runners instead of racing a legitimate timeout.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(eventloops): simplify waiter wake delivery

Keep `PollWakeReason` latched on the waiter itself and go back to the
plain `schedule(task)` ownership handoff. This removes the unnecessary
use of `schedule(task, reason)` and `wait()` return values while
preserving the ready-versus-canceled wake semantics added for poller
waits.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(slave): group runtime and protocol internals

Summary:
- move internal slave FSM helpers under Slave.Runtime and
  process-data helpers under Slave.ProcessData
- move CoE under Slave.Mailbox and ESC register/SII helpers under
  Slave.ESC
- update examples, tests, and architecture docs to match the new
  slave namespace layout

Rationale:
- the flat slave folder made internal helpers look like peer
  subsystems, which made the boundary harder to scan
- grouping by runtime, process-data, mailbox, and ESC concerns makes
  the top-level slave structure reflect the actual protocol areas
- pre-release API rules allow tightening these names now instead of
  carrying a muddled structure forward

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(planning): 同步 Cycle 5 RFC 与工作流基线

Summary:
- 更新 WORKFLOW 的 Linear 项目与分支命名配置，并补充 issue 描述注入
- 补齐 Cycle 5 RFC 草案，包括 PR Merge Gating 与后续扩展规划
- 更新扩展池索引并移除 linear skill 文档

Rationale:
- 先统一扩展规划和 PR merge gating 基线，避免后续 agent 各自实现
- 让当前 workflow 与规划文档保持一致，便于后续直接分发任务

Tests:
- go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: preserve survival wave starting ball

Major Features:
- Survival wave recovery
  - Promote a surviving ball when the primary ball is lost during multiball
  - Recreate and attach a replacement main ball when a new wave would otherwise start without one

Code Improvements:
- Survival transition handling
  - Resolve the preserved ball explicitly before clearing extra balls during wave transitions
  - Filter queued-for-deletion balls out of active-ball discovery to avoid stale references

Documentation:
- Runtime architecture notes
  - Document the primary-ball ownership invariant for multiball and survival transitions

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Deploy Anvish website

Add a separate website repository with a Monaco editor, browser-side
WASI runner, GitHub Pages workflow, and local build script. The site
compiles a small web runner against the core Anvish repository, serves
a modern single-page UI, and publishes as a static site on github.io.
Validation:
- ./scripts/build-site.sh
- cargo test --quiet in ../anvish
